specialist_agents.purpose: Review authentication, authorization, secret handling, and security-sensitive changes.
Review UI changes for semantics, keyboard access, labels, focus, and regression risk.
Review public API and protocol compatibility risks.
specialist_agents.trigger: public API compatibility indicators were present

specialist_agents.purpose: Review dependency upgrade, lockfile, and package manager risk.
specialist_agents.trigger: dependency or lockfile indicators were present

reasoning: Classified task as huge with whole_repo scope and critical risk.
Goal appears read-only, so staffing favors scouts, reviewers, testers, and reporters over executors.
Repository index reports 243 indexed file(s); 24 candidate relevant file(s) were selected.

Created dynamic specialists only for matched evidence: AuthSecurityReviewerAgent: security/authentication indicators were present; AccessibilityReviewerAgent: frontend/accessibility indicators were present; APICompatibilityReviewerAgent: public API compatibility indicators were present; DependencyUpgradeReviewerAgent: dependency or lockfile indicators were present.
The system selected 300 logical agents automatically; the user did not need to provide an agent count.
downgrade_conditions: Reduce executor fan-out after any validation failure or repeated patch fingerprint.
Downgrade to fewer scouts when the repo index is fresh and relevant files are already narrow.
Use a single executor when write scopes overlap or sensitive files are involved.
escalation_conditions: Escalate scouts and reviewers when relevant modules remain unknown.
Add specialists when security, database, API, dependency, performance, or UI evidence appears.
Require human approval when high-risk files or critical safety gates are involved.
